sourcePriority: STATdx supplemental depth, cases, and visual reinforcement

sourceCoverage.textCoverage: STATdx substantially duplicates the RadPrimer article structure and adds small modality/mechanism details plus a broader image set.

sourceCoverage.imageCoverageSummary: All 10 RadPrimer images have visually exact STATdx duplicate copies in the staged evidence set; the RadPrimer versions remain canonical, and STATdx duplicate copies are archived.

sourceAttributionRules: Use [RadPrimer] for hierarchy, canonical definitions, and RadPrimer captions/images.
Use [STATdx] for supplemental cases, modality-specific examples, and STATdx-only caption detail.
Use [Both] only for fused statements explicitly supported by both provided source packages.
Use clean human-facing image labels in package prose; keep short codes in registry, manifest, filenames, and traceability fields.

sourceSelectionPlan.textBackbone: Use RadPrimer hierarchy and article organization as the canonical backbone because RadPrimer is present and supplies the target Anki breadcrumb.
textKeepFromRadPrimer: Core classification framework: otic-capsule violating versus sparing, plus older longitudinal/transverse/oblique descriptors.
Systematic checklist for CNVII canal, otic capsule, tegmen, carotid canal, ossicles, intracranial contents, and cervical spine.
Clinical management/prognosis points about CNVII injury, CSF leak, vascular injury, and chronic complications.
textKeepFromSTATdx: Supplemental detail that OCV fractures more often affect labyrinthine structures, including cochlea, vestibule, and semicircular canals.
Penetrating-trauma/ossicular-chain examples and extensive visual examples of vascular, pseudofracture, chronic, and MR complications.
Additional modality-specific captions for CTA, MR, oblique reformations, and same-patient CT/MR complication clusters.
textDownweightOrSkip: Do not duplicate identical RadPrimer/STATdx article prose when both sources say the same thing.
Do not treat Core Radiology as verified because no Core source package is present.
Do not generate cards or lecture content from this package yet.
sourceSelectionPlan.imageCurationPolicy: Exact duplicates may be archived. Near duplicates, alternate same-disease examples, conceptual replacements, different patients, different projections, adjacent slices, or modality variants remain selected as recognition reinforcement unless visually identical or unusable.
sourceSelectionPlan.duplicateEvidencePolicy: Exact duplicate decisions require visual evidence from image_evidence files, same stable source image ID, matching hashes, or explicit manual/source confirmation. Caption-only or topic-only similarity is not enough; uncertain cases stay primary.

caseClusterGuardrails.atomicClusterRule: Same-patient, follow-up, procedure, comparison-view, adjacent-slice, time-lapse, and CT/MR companion groups stay together when selected.
caseClusterGuardrails.splitPolicy: No source case cluster was intentionally split. Duplicate STATdx tail images were archived only because the equivalent RadPrimer cluster images remain selected.

imageDownloadPlan.filenamePolicy: Use source-qualified filenames from image_registry.downloadFilename, preserving short IDs such as RP-05 and SDX-04 in filenames/traceability while using clean labels in prose.
imageDownloadPlan.extensionInstruction: Default image import should download only primaryTeachingSet images and skip archiveOptionalDuplicate images unless the user explicitly requests complete-source recovery.

generatorInstructionsForNarrativeAndCard: Use source-qualified human labels in narrative, for example RadPrimer image 5 or STATdx image 4.
Do not force both the long human label and short code into the same narrative-facing sentence; keep RP-05/SDX-04 in traceability fields.
Use RadPrimer images as the canonical backbone, then add selected STATdx images as supplemental reinforcement for visual variety and complications.
Preserve atomic case clusters when selecting image prompts or cards.
